description: 8 tests with verifiable correct answers — math, logic, code analysis, architecture

questions.question:
Solve step by step, then give the final answer on its own line.

A company has 3 departments. Engineering has 10 people. Sales has 2x Engineering (20 people). Marketing has half of Sales (10 people). Each person costs $80,000/year. What is the total annual payroll? Show your math.

Solve this logic chain step by step:

Rule 1: if X then Y
Rule 2: if Y then not Z
Rule 3: if not Z then W

Given: X is true.

What is the value of W? Show each step.

System must handle 10,000 req/sec with 99.9% uptime. Options:
A) Single powerful server ($5k/mo)
B) Load-balanced cluster of 4 servers ($8k/mo)
C) Serverless (pay per request)

Which option and why? Consider cost, reliability, and latency tradeoffs.
Name the specific logical fallacy in one sentence: "Our product has 10,000 users. Zero security complaints. Therefore it is secure."
A developer added an in-memory cache: `cache = {}` with no TTL and no max size. API responses are cached by URL key. List exactly 3 problems, one per line.
Table has 1M rows with 20 individual single-column indexes. Queries always filter on 2-3 columns together. In 2 sentences, what is wrong and how to fix it?
A farmer has chickens and cows. Total animals: 15. Total legs: 44. How many chickens? Show your work, then state the final answer clearly.

metadata.author: crucibulum-core
metadata.diagnostic_purpose: Tests verifiable reasoning across math, logic chains, code bug detection, architecture decisions, and logical fallacies
